name: algolia-search
description: Expert patterns for Algolia search implementation, indexing strategies, React InstantSearch, and relevance tuning Use when: adding search to, algolia, instantsearch, search api, search functionality.
capabilities: Set up React InstantSearch with modern hooks (useSearchBox, useHits, useRefinementList)
Configure Next.js server-side rendering with InstantSearchNext component
Design indexing strategies including full reindexing, record updates, and partial updates
Implement faceted search with refinement lists and filters
Optimize batch indexing with proper record sizing (10MB, 1K-10K records per batch)
Tune search relevance with Algolia ranking settings
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 3 patterns as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The flagged lines contain documentation text about Algolia search implementation, not executable code. Line 3 and 70 contain descriptive text misidentified as weak crypto. Line 53 discusses Algolia indexing best practices, not system reconnaissance. This is a documentation-only skill file with no security risks.
valueStatement: Adding search to web applications requires understanding complex indexing strategies and UI patterns. This skill provides expert guidance on Algolia integration, React InstantSearch hooks, and relevance tuning for optimal search experiences.
userTitle: Implement Algolia Search with React InstantSearch
limitations: Does not provide actual API credentials or account setup - users must configure their own Algolia account
Focuses on React and Next.js implementations, not other frameworks
Does not cover advanced Algolia features like Query Rules or A/B testing
Requires existing Algolia index with properly structured data

useCases.title: E-commerce Product Search
useCases.description: Implement fast, faceted product search with category filters, price ranges, and instant results for online stores.

useCases.targetUser: Technical writers and developer experience engineers
useCases.title: Documentation Site Search
useCases.description: Add type-ahead search to documentation sites with relevance tuning for technical content and API references.

useCases.title: Content Discovery Portal
useCases.description: Build searchable content hubs with pagination, sorting, and multi-index search for blogs or media libraries.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Search Component Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a React search component using Algolia InstantSearch hooks. Include a search box, hits display, and basic styling for a simple product catalog.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Setting up your first Algolia search interface

promptTemplates.title: Faceted Search with Filters
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement faceted search with refinement lists for categories, price ranges, and brand filters. Use useRefinementList and configure the Algolia index settings for faceting.

promptTemplates.title: Next.js SSR Search Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Algolia InstantSearch with Next.js server-side rendering using the InstantSearchNext component. Include URL synchronization and initial server state handling for an App Router application.

promptTemplates.title: Incremental Index Update Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an indexing strategy for keeping Algolia synchronized with a PostgreSQL database. Implement partialUpdateObjects for attribute-only changes and batch records efficiently without using deleteBy.

bestPractices: Batch index operations with 10MB or 1K-10K records per batch for optimal performance
Use partialUpdateObjects instead of full record replacement when only updating specific attributes
Avoid deleteBy operations as they are computationally expensive - use object deletion with batch instead
antiPatterns: Performing full reindexing on every data change instead of incremental updates
Sending individual records to Algolia instead of batching operations
Using deleteBy for bulk deletions instead of batch object deletion

faq.question: What is the difference between full reindexing and partial updates?
faq.answer: Full reindexing replaces the entire index, which is expensive and slow. Partial updates modify only specific attributes of existing records using partialUpdateObjects, which is much more efficient for frequent changes.

faq.question: How do I enable server-side rendering for Algolia in Next.js?
faq.answer: Use the react-instantsearch-nextjs package and wrap your search components with InstantSearchNext instead of InstantSearch. Configure getServerState for initial state and set dynamic = 'force-dynamic' for fresh results.

faq.question: What is the optimal batch size for indexing?
faq.answer: The ideal batch size is 10MB or approximately 1K-10K records per batch. This balances API performance with memory usage and avoids timeout issues.

faq.question: Why should I avoid the deleteBy method?
faq.answer: deleteBy is computationally expensive because it scans and matches records before deletion. For bulk deletions, it is more efficient to fetch object IDs and use batch delete operations.

faq.question: Which React hooks are available for InstantSearch?
faq.answer: Key hooks include useSearchBox for search input, useHits for results, useRefinementList for facet filters, usePagination for page navigation, and useInstantSearch for full state access.

faq.question: Can I use Algolia with the Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes, react-instantsearch-nextjs supports both Pages Router and App Router. App Router support is marked as experimental but functional with proper configuration of server components and streaming.
